pattern: When k is large enough to allow all profitable moves, a greedy pass suffices; otherwise a DP table tracking best profit per transaction count avoids redundant recomputation.
hints: If you can make at most k transactions and k exceeds half the number of days, unlimited transactions are effectively allowed — just collect every upward day.
Otherwise model the state as (transactions used, day): dp[i][j] is the maximum profit using at most i transactions in the first j days.
For each transaction count i, carry a running variable representing the best 'buy-low' opportunity seen so far to update dp[i][j] in O(1) per cell.

edge_inputs.description: k=2, prices strictly increasing, two transactions cover all
single day, cannot complete any transaction
k >= n/2 so unlimited transactions apply, greedy path must be taken
k=1 with best buy early and sell late

stress.description: random prices of varying length with k between 1 and n/2+5
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    prices = [rng.randrange(0, 1001) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(1, max(2, n // 2 + 5))
    return [k, prices]
